reports.title: claude-code
reports.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows - all through natural language commands.

reports.title: Data Driven NYC - Can One Person Build a Billion-Dollar Startup?
reports.summary: Andrew from The General Intelligence Company presents CTO, a fully autonomous engineering department that can code, test, and deploy features autonomously using AI agents. The system uses Claude for code generation, browser agents for testing, and Vercel for deployment. Teams using CTO average 25 PRs per engineer per day.
reports.name: Data Driven NYC
reports.summary_en: Andrew from The General Intelligence Company presents CTO, a fully autonomous engineering department that can code, test, and deploy features autonomously using AI agents.

reports.title: Aaron Levie (CEO @Box) on X
reports.summary: Box CEO Aaron Levie argues that the ultimate rate limiter on AI productivity gains will be security, compliance, and governance—not technology. Companies won't take on every risk of every piece of AI-generated code in enterprise environments.
reports.name: Aaron Levie
reports.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

tweets.text:
The ultimate rate limiter on productivity gains from agents will be on critical stuff like security, compliance, governance, the ability to review the work of the agent, ensure that it's compatible with regulations, and so on.

We've been living in a little bit of la-la land around how much software enterprises are going to ultimately want to vibe code themselves. The last 48 hours represents a good example of why you won't take on every risk of every piece of technology in your enterprise.

There's no free lunch with AI productivity.
tweets.text_en: The ultimate rate limiter on productivity gains from agents will be on critical stuff like security, compliance, governance...

reports.summary_en: Box CEO Aaron Levie argues that security, compliance, and governance—not technology—will be the ultimate rate limiter on AI productivity gains in enterprise.

reports.title: Aditya Agarwal (GP @SouthPkCommons) on X
reports.summary: Aditya Agarwal (Ex-Facebook/Dropbox, GP @SouthPkCommons) muses that the modern version of Facebook's 'fixing bugs in production' philosophy might be making source code public, given how dynamic software has become.
reports.name: Aditya Agarwal
reports.bio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart

tweets.text:
Looking back, early Facebook's philosophy of 'fixing bugs in production' sounded crazy to most people.

Maybe the modern version of that is, just let your source code be public.

If software is so dynamic, maybe this isn't so crazy.

Or maybe it is totally batshit crazy.
tweets.text_en: Looking back, early Facebook's philosophy of 'fixing bugs in production' sounded crazy... Maybe the modern version is just let your source code be public.

reports.summary_en: Aditya Agarwal muses that making source code public might be the modern equivalent of 'fixing bugs in production'.
